Test05-OCT-2016Specimen

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine), with low total CO₂ suggesting possible mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low HbA1c is unusual given glucosuria; consider analytical error or recent changes in glycemic control. Findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology; recommend clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation.
